title: In Person Crisis Intervention - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > In Person Crisis Intervention 40 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Disaster/Emergency Issues BENTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH BENTON COUNTY CRISIS CENTER MOBILE CRISIS TEAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assesses individuals in the community through a mobile crisis team upon request from various community partners, including law enforcement. 240 NW 4th Street Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Individuals who are experiencing a mental health crisis Hours: 24 hours per day/ 7 days per week (541) 766-6767 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADIA HEALTH CASCADIA PLAZA HEALTH CENTER MENTAL HEALTH URGENT WALK-IN CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides care to those experiencing a behavioral health crisis. Services are based on clinical need and may include counseling, prescribing medications, and referrals to affordable mental health, medical, and substance treatment and other community resources. 4212 SE Division Street Suites 100 and 200 Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 7am-9pm, Saturday-Sunday 9am-9pm Email (503) 963-2575 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DIVISION 24/7 MOBILE CRISIS RESPONSE TEAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-person therapeutic responses as well as peer support services in public settings where a mental health crisis is occurring, including an individual's home, school, business and on the street. Mobile crisis teams consist of clinicians, care coordinators and peer support specialists. 2051 Kaen Road Suite 154 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 655-8585 247 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DIVISION CLACKAMAS MENTAL HEALTH CENTER CRISIS AND URGENT WALK-IN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an urgent mental health walk-in center. Offers crisis stabilization services including counselors, peer support, case management, crisis intake assessment, referral to medical services and general resources. 11211 SE 82nd Avenue Suite O Happy Valley, OR 97086 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis and Support line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Walk-in Mental Health Center: Monday-Friday 9am-7pm Mobile Crisis Response: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 655-8585 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIA COMMUNITY MENTAL HEALTH COLUMBIA COMMUNITY MENTAL HEALTH CREEKSIDE CENTER CCMH CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF County crisis line providing support for people experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. Crisis therapists provide supportive counseling, outreach, assessment, and referral information to individuals experiencing an emotional crisis or interpersonal difficulties. Offers immediate, short-term help to individuals who experience an event that produces emotional, mental, physical, and behavioral distress or problems. 58646 McNulty Way Street Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 397-5211 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY COUNSELING SOLUTIONS ARLINGTON BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers mental health services for both adults and children. Services include individual and group therapy, mental health assessment, case management with other service providers, and medication management when necessary. 120 Arlington Mall Street Arlington, OR 97812 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-3pm (541) 454-2223 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY COUNSELING SOLUTIONS BOARDMAN BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers mental health services for both adults and children. Services include individual and group therapy, mental health assessment, case management with other service providers, and medication management when necessary. 104 SW Kinkade Road Boardman, OR 97818 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-3pm (541) 481-2911 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY COUNSELING SOLUTIONS COMMUNITY COUNSELING SOLUTIONS PENDLETON MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers mental health services for both adults and children. Services include individual and group therapy, mental health assessment, case management with other service providers, and medication management when necessary. 331 SE 2nd Street Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-3pm (541) 276-6207 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY COUNSELING SOLUTIONS CONDON BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers mental health services for both adults and children. Services include individual and group therapy, mental health assessment, case management with other service providers, and medication management when necessary. 422 N Main Street Condon, OR 97823 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-3pm (541) 384-2666 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY COUNSELING SOLUTIONS FOSSIL BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers mental health services for both adults and children. Services include individual and group therapy, mental health assessment, case management with other service providers, and medication management when necessary. 401 4th Street Fossil, OR 97830 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-3pm (541) 763-2746 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY COUNSELING SOLUTIONS GRANT COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers mental health services for both adults and children. Services include individual and group therapy, mental health assessment, case management with other service providers, and medication management when necessary. 528 East Main Street Suite W John Day, OR 97845 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-3pm (541) 575-1466 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY COUNSELING SOLUTIONS HEPPNER BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers mental health services for both adults and children. Services include individual and group therapy, mental health assessment, case management with other service providers, and medication management when necessary. 550 West Sperry Street Heppner, OR 97836 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-3pm Email (541) 676-9161 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY COUNSELING SOLUTIONS HERMISTON MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers mental health services for both adults and children. Services include individual and group therapy, mental health assessment, case management with other service providers, and medication management when necessary. 595 NW 11th Street Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-3pm (541) 567-2536 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY COUNSELING SOLUTIONS MILTON-FREEWATER MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers mental health services for both adults and children. Services include individual and group therapy, mental health assessment, case management with other service providers, and medication management when necessary. 299 N Columbia Milton Freewater, OR 97862 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-3pm (541) 276-6207 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COOS HEALTH AND WELLNESS COOS COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for people experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. A mobile response team can be activated by contacting the crisis line. 281 LaClair Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 266-6800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DESCHUTES COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DESCHUTES COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for people experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. Crisis services are provided to any individual in need; insurance coverage is not required. Services may include assessment, intervention planning, and information and referral services. Brief crisis stabilization through individual or group treatment may also be provided. 2577 NE Courtney Drive Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Walk in: Monday-Friday 8:30am-3:30pm Email (541) 322-7500 9 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH JACKSON COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. Crisis services include risk assessment, safety planning and coordination with community resources and services. In person crisis intervention services are available. 140 S Holly Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: In-person crisis services: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm All other serivces: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 774-8201 24 7 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH BASIN BEHAVIORAL HEALTH KLAMATH BASIN BEHAVIORAL HEALTH ELDORADO MOBILE CRISIS SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Responds to individuals experiencing a mental health crisis by providing a mental health assessment to facilitate connection to the most appropriate community resource. Phone support, co-response, and in-person response services are available. 2210 Eldorado Avenue Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Walk-in crisis services: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 883-1030 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH BASIN BEHAVIORAL HEALTH KLAMATH BASIN BEHAVIORAL HEALTH ELDORADO OUTPATIENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Child, Adolescent, and Family Outpatient Services work with children presenting with problems of behavior, anxiety, depression, psychotic thinking, and trauma-related issues. Services include individual, family, and group therapy that focuses on strategic and structural family issues and support, trauma-related care, and wraparound services (for high-need children). 2210 Eldorado Avenue Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Age 18 and younger Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5pm Email (541) 883-1030 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH BASIN BEHAVIORAL HEALTH KLAMATH BASIN BEHAVIORAL HEALTH WASHBURN MOBILE CRISIS SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Responds to individuals experiencing a mental health crisis by providing a mental health assessment to facilitate connection to the most appropriate community resource. Phone support, co-response, and in-person response services are available. 725 Washburn Way Klamath Falls, OR 97603 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Walk-in crisis services: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 883-1030 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH BASIN BEHAVIORAL HEALTH KLAMATH BASIN BEHAVIORAL HEALTH WASHBURN PHOENIX PLACE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides respite, residential care, and treatment for adults experiencing emotional and mental health crisis. 725 Washburn Way Klamath Falls, OR 97603 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 883-1030 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LANE COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MOBILE CRISIS SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers mobile crisis response for mental health issues. Provides community-based response to deescalate and manage acute crises. Also offers follow-up care and coordination to ensure ongoing support. 2411 Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Individuals in crisis Hours: Monday-Friday 6am-11pm, Saturday-Sunday 2pm-11pm (541) 682-1001 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINN COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH LINN COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH ALBANY LINN COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for people experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. Offers initial crisis screening assessment, telephone triage, crisis intervention, pre-commitment investigation, and hospital liaison services. Also offers in-person crisis intervention services during the day. 445 3rd Avenue SW Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week In-person crisis services: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (541) 967-3866 ( 1) Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARION COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MARION COUNTY CHILDREN'S BEHAVIORAL HEALTH YOUTH & FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Children's Behavioral Health Services work with children presenting with problems of behavior, anxiety, depression, psychotic thinking, and trauma related issues. Services include individual, family, and group therapy that focuses on strategic and structural family issues and support, trauma-related care, and wraparound services (for high needs children). 3867 Wolverine Street NE Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Ages 0-18 Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 8:30am-7pm Wednesday/Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 588-5352 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARION COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MARION COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH PSYCHIATRIC CRISIS CENTER BEHAVIORAL HEALTH CRISIS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Behavioral Health Crisis Center (BHCC) provides in-person crisis intervention. Services range from stabilization, mental health screening, hospitalization, respite and referrals for on-going case management, as well as short-term emergency prescriptions. At least one Qualified Mental Health Professional (QMHP) is on duty at all times to provide face-to-face assessments and crisis counseling. 1234 Commercial Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Adults experiencing a mental health crisis Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 585-4949 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARION COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MARION COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH WOODBURN BEHAVIORAL HEALTH CRISIS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Behavioral Health Crisis Center (BHCC) provides in-person crisis intervention. Services range from stabilization, mental health screening, hospitalization, respite and referrals for on-going case management, as well as short-term emergency prescriptions. At least one Qualified Mental Health Professional (QMHP) is on duty at all times to provide face-to-face assessments and crisis counseling. 976 N Pacific Highway Woodburn, OR 97071 Eligibility: Adults experiencing a mental health crisis Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 585-4949 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-COLUMBIA CENTER FOR LIVING MID-COLUMBIA CENTER FOR LIVING DROP IN CENTER MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health services for children, youth, and adults, including mental health diagnosis and assessment, individual and group therapy, case management, screening and referrals to inpatient or residential services as needed, and community education. 1060 Webber Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-7pm, Saturday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 296-5452 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MULTNOMAH COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports people in crisis with mental health issues, suicidal thoughts, or emergencies. Provides phone counseling, guidance on next steps, and can dispatch a mobile team or arrange transport to a care facility. Also offers referrals to mental health and addiction treatment services. Also serves as the front door to Multnomah Behavioral Health Services (mental health and substance use disorder), the behavioral health benefit for Oregon Health Plan members in Multnomah County assigned to Health Share of Oregon CCO. 209 SW 4th Avenue Suite 520 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 988-5887 Get Directions Visit (503) 623-9289 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RIVERVIEW CENTER FOR GROWTH MOBILE RESPONSE AND STABILIZATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support to children experiencing a mental health crisis. Program crisis workers can provide assistance and referrals to help stabilize the situation, and create a short-term plan. On-site services available for more intensive intervention needs. The team may seek authorization for a brief, voluntary crisis care placement, approved by the parent. 3995 Marcola Road Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Families with youth age 20 and younger who are experiencing a mental health crisis Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (888) 989-9990 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. 990 NW Brooks Street Suite 1 Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 389-7021 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE MADRAS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 475-1880 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE PRINEVILLE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 416-2114 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE REDMOND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 504-2550 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SYMMETRY CARE INC HARNEY COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 348 W Adams Street Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Walk-in: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 573-8376 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRAUMA INTERVENTION PROGRAM NW Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Trained and screened volunteers provide emotional and practical support to people involved in traumatic events and their families in the first few hours following a tragedy: complete follow-up calls, information, and referral to other resources (burial services, probate information, hazardous clean up, and other services). Volunteers are requested to crisis scenes by police officers, firefighters, hospital personnel, organizations/businesses, and schools after a traumatic event. Eligibility: People involved in traumatic (i.e., an unexpected death) or crisis events and their families Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 940-7997 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITY CENTER FOR BEHAVIORAL HEALTH INPATIENT AND EMERGENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides psychiatric inpatient services for youth and adults, and a Psychiatric Emergency Service (PES). On-site representatives from various community partners and peer support personnel offer transitional support services. 1225 NE 2nd Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Inpatient: Ages 9-70 PES: Age 18 and older Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 944-8000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WASHINGTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH HILLSBORO RECOVERY CENTER HAWTHORN WALK-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides urgent care services for mental health and addiction concerns. Services include assessment for mental health and/or addiction concerns, crisis counseling, information and referral, education on mental health and addiction, peer support, and connection to treatment providers. Concerned loved ones may also receive support and information about available community services. 5250 NE Elam Young Parkway Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday 10am-4pm (503) 846-2288 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WESTERN LANE CRISIS RESPONSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-person crisis services including de-escalation, suicide risk assessment, safety planning, and resource navingation. Also offers follow-up support for mental health, substance use, and other life crises. 2625 Highway 101 Florence, OR 97439 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 997-3515 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WHITE BIRD CLINIC CAHOOTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mobile crisis intervention services. Responds to a range of non-criminal crises, including intoxication, disorientation, substance use and mental health issues, dispute resolution, and street facilitation. Transport to treatment is also provided. Service is confidential, voluntary, and staffed and managed by White Bird Clinic. Home visits available. 323 E 12th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: People who require crisis intervention Hours: 7 days per week 11am-11pm; service hours may vary depending on staff availability
